rulings.Case Title: MARK B HOFFMAN VS. ANN FAY BARRY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, AUGUST 25, 2008, LINE 8 PLAINTIFF MARK HOFFMAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. THE MOTION IS GRANTED TO CONFORM THE COMPLAINT TO THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MARK B HOFFMAN VS. ANN FAY BARRY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, AUGUST 25, 2008, LINE 8 DEFENDANT ANN BARRY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM WAS TIMELY FILED BASED ON THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED. THE 3 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATION, CCP 338, CONTROLS THE NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CLAIM. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED THE GRAVERMAN OF THE COMPLAINT SOUNDS IN FRAUD. THIS IN NOT A CASE OF PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE AS IN VENTURA COUNTY NATIONAL BANK V. MARKER (1996) 49 CAL. APP 4TH 1528, NOR IS CIVIL CODE 2070 APPLICABLE FOR DEFENDANT IS NOT A REAL ESTATE BROKER. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. ON THE ISSUE OF RELIANCE, TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ARE PRESENTED GIVEN THE REQUIREMENT THAT REASONABLE INFLUENCES BE CONSTRUED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF. THE INDEPENDENT INVESTIGATION BY PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PRECLUDE A FINDING OF RELIANCE. BAGDASARIAN V. GRAYNON (1948) 31 C. 2ND 744, 748 =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES A. BLANCO VS. AMERICAN BOARD OF FORENSIC DOCUMENT EXAMINERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, AUGUST 25, 2008, LINE 14 DEFENDANT AMERICAN BOARD OF FORENSIC DOCUMENT EXAMINERS' Second Amended Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR ASSERTING JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. THE FACTS IN THIS CASE ARE ANALOGOUS TO THE DECISION IN FONTANETTA V. AMERICAN BOARD OF INTERNAL MEDICINE 2ND CIR. (1970) 421 F.2ND 355. THE MOTION IS TIMELY. OLINICK V. BMG ENTERTAINMENT (2006) 138 CAL. APP 4TH 1286, 1295-96. =(302/PJM/VC)
